title: Quantum fluctuations in quartet superfluid of two-dimensional Fermi mixture
authors: Wei Wang, Yupeng Wang, Xiaoling Cui
abstract: We study quantum fluctuations in quartet superfluid (QSF) of two-dimensional (2D) fermion mixtures with mass imbalance. Here QSF is a high-order superfluid that corresponds to the condensation of ($1+3$) clusters, each consisting of a light fermion and three heavy ones. By incorporating the Gaussian fluctuations respecting dominant four-body correlations in this system, our theory successfully produces the logarithmic dependence of the 2D equation of state in the deep binding regime, thereby offering a correct physical picture of quartet clusters behaving as composite bosons. By extending the Gaussian fluctuation theory from pairing to quartet superfluids, our results shed light on quantum fluctuations in general fermion superfluids with arbitrarily high-order correlations.
summary: This paper investigates quantum fluctuations in a 2D quartet superfluid made of one light and three heavy fermions. It demonstrates that including Gaussian fluctuations correctly recovers the expected bosonic equation of state in the deep binding limit. The work establishes a general framework for studying higher-order fermion correlations.
main_problem: The study aims to understand quantum fluctuations in a quartet superfluid (QSF) of 2D mass-imbalanced Fermi mixtures and to determine if these clusters behave as composite bosons in the deep binding limit.
main_result: The theory successfully reproduces the logarithmic dependence of the 2D equation of state in the deep binding regime, confirming that quartet clusters behave as composite bosons.
method: The authors use a functional path integral approach with an extended Gaussian fluctuation theory that incorporates dominant four-body correlations.
model_or_system: A two-dimensional (2D) Fermi mixture with mass imbalance (heavy and light fermions) in a (1+3) cluster configuration, specifically considering realistic examples like 6Li-40K and 6Li-53Cr.
key_observables: Thermodynamic potential (Omega), equation of state (EoS), gap equation, number equations, and the quartet chemical potential.
important_parameters: Mass imbalance (mh, ml), 2D scattering length (a), quartet binding energy (E4), and the deep binding limit (reduced chemical potential approaching zero).
main_assumptions: The study focuses on the zero-temperature (T=0) ground state and assumes a specific heavy-to-light number ratio of 3:1.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows diagrammatic representations of Green's functions and fluctuation matrix elements; Figure 2 plots the thermodynamic potential as a function of the logarithm of the reduced chemical potential for different mass-imbalanced systems.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of quartet superfluidity, presents a mean-field theory for (1+3) clusters, extends this to Gaussian fluctuation theory, provides analytical results for the deep binding limit, and validates the theory with numerical simulations.
why_interesting: It provides a theoretical framework for extending fluctuation theories from standard pairing to higher-order correlations, which is highly relevant for understanding many-body dynamics and complex cluster formation in ultracold gases.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Diagrammatic representation of the Green’s func- tions (a) and typical elements in the fluctuation matrix M (b1-d2). In (a), we use a double blue (bold red) line with an arrow to denote the mean-field Green’s function G0 11 (G0 22) for the heavy (light) fermion, and the lines with two arrows repre- sent the mean-field anomalous Green’s functions G0 12 and G0 21. The dashed line represents a quartet fluctuation field with momentum q, within which the two unpaired heavy fermions carry momenta k2 and k3 (as labeled in the figure). (b1) and (b2) correspond to the elements in M11 and M21 when the two momenta {k2k3} are kept unchanged. These are also the typ- ical diagrams for quantum...

FIG. 2. Thermodynamic potential of QSF from quantum fluctuations (ΩQF) as a function of ln(˜µQ), with ˜µQ being the reduced chemical potential of quartet bosons (see defi- nition in Eq.(26)). The solid and dashed lines correspond to numerical results based on Eq.(25) and an analytical fit based on Eq.(27), respectively. The black and gray colors respectively denote 6Li-40K and 6Li-53Cr systems with differ- ent heavy-light mass ratios. Here we take the energy unit as E2b = 1/(2mra2), and denote ˜S = S/a2.
